footnotes.text: Moses permitted us . . . to put her away: the Mosaic Law permitted divorce for certain causes. In the verses immediately following here, Jesus abrogates divorce and declares the indissolubility of marriage.
It is easier. . . : Jesus again speaks figuratively. He does not condemn riches as essentially evil. A rich man who is detached from his riches may be saved, with the grace of God.
Relatives are not to be a stumbling-block to salvation. But Jesus does not teach the complete severance of the bonds of physical relationship.
The sons of Zebedee had not yet grasped the spiritual nature of the kingdom of God.
